hero.headline: Know the real cost
hero.subline: Calculate the price per use of anything in seconds. Try free for 3 days. No ads.

features.title: Everything you need to
features.subtitle: A complete toolkit to evaluate any purchase, compare options, and never overpay again.
features.costPerUseDesc: Enter any price, estimate how often you'll use it, and instantly see the true cost per use.
features.compareDesc: Put two items side by side and find out which one gives you more value for your money.
features.benchmarksDesc: 33 built-in categories with real-world benchmarks so you know if your cost per use is good or bad. Plus, create your own custom categories.
features.savedDesc: Keep a library of your past calculations. Attach photos so you remember exactly what you evaluated.
features.shareDesc: Generate beautiful share cards with your cost-per-use verdict and post them anywhere.
features.noSubDesc: Try every feature free for 3 days. Then just £3.99/week or save with £39.99/year. The app pays for itself with a single smarter purchase.
features.worthItDateDesc: See exactly when your purchase pays for itself. Track the countdown to your break-even point and know the moment it becomes worth every penny.
features.languagesDesc: Available in English, Spanish, French, German, Italian, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and Traditional Chinese — shop smarter in your language.
features.logUseDesc: Log every use and watch your cost per use drop in real time. Your "Worth It" date automatically adjusts as your habits change.

pricing.title: Start saving
pricing.subtitle: Try free for 3 days. One skipped impulse buy pays for a whole year.

faq.title: Frequently asked
faq.a1: Cost per use is a simple formula: the total price of an item divided by the number of times you use it. It tells you the real value of a purchase — a £200 jacket worn 200 times costs just £1 per use, while a £50 jacket worn 5 times costs £10 per use. Cost per use helps you see past the price tag.
faq.a2: Skip or Buy is free to try for 3 days. After that, it's £3.99/week or £39.99/year. Most users save that in their first week — just one smarter purchase decision covers the cost. You can cancel anytime.
faq.a3: The app includes 33 built-in categories covering everything from clothing and footwear to electronics, kitchen items, furniture, fitness gear, beauty products, and more. Each category comes with real-world benchmarks so you can see how your cost per use compares. You can also create your own custom categories to match your lifestyle.
faq.a4: No. The app works completely offline. All calculations happen on your device. You don't need an internet connection to use any feature.
faq.q5: Is my data stored in the cloud?
faq.a5: No. All your data — saved items, photos, calculations — is stored locally on your device. We don't have accounts, cloud storage, or any way to access your data. Your information stays on your phone.

cta.title: Stop guessing.
cta.subtitle: Download Skip or Buy: Cost Per Use and make every purchase count.

cookie.message: We use anonymous analytics to improve the app experience. No personal data is collected.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Skip or Buy: Cost Per Use ("the App") is developed and maintained by Skip Or Buy ("we", "us", "our"). We are committed to protecting your privacy. This policy explains what data the App collects (or doesn't) and how it is handled.
privacy.dataStorage: All data you create in the App — including saved items, photos, calculations, and preferences — is stored locally on your device. We do not operate any cloud storage, user accounts, or synchronisation services. Your data never leaves your phone.
privacy.analyticsIntro: We use Firebase Analytics, a privacy-focused and GDPR-compliant analytics service, to collect anonymous usage statistics. This helps us understand how the App is used so we can improve it. Firebase Analytics does not collect personally identifiable information. The data collected includes:
privacy.analyticsItem2: Feature usage counts (e.g., how often calculations are made)
privacy.analyticsItem5: Country-level location (derived from locale, not GPS)
privacy.analyticsOutro: No personal data, names, email addresses, IP addresses, or device identifiers are collected.
privacy.camera: The App may request access to your camera or photo library so you can attach photos to saved items. These photos are stored locally on your device only. We never upload, transmit, or access your photos in any way.
privacy.thirdParty: The App does not include any advertising SDKs, tracking pixels, social media SDKs, or third-party data-sharing integrations. The only third-party service used is Firebase Analytics for anonymous analytics as described above.
privacy.dataSharing: We do not sell, rent, trade, or share your data with any third party. Since all user data is stored locally on your device, we have no access to it.
privacy.children: The App is not directed at children under 13. We do not knowingly collect any personal information from children.
privacy.deletion: Since all data is stored locally on your device, you can delete it at any time by clearing the App's data in your device settings or by uninstalling the App.
privacy.changes: We may update this privacy policy from time to time. Any changes will be reflected on this page with an updated "Last updated" date.
privacy.contactIntro: If you have any questions about this privacy policy, please contact us at:

problemBridge.title: The price tag lies.
problemBridge.titleAccent: The cost per use tells the truth.
problemBridge.subtitle: Two purchases. Two very different stories. Tap to reveal what they really cost.
problemBridge.goodDetail: Worn 3 times a week for 2 years. That is 312 wears from a single purchase.
problemBridge.badDetail: Used twice, then forgotten in the back of a cupboard.
problemBridge.closingLine: The expensive one was the bargain. The cheap one was the waste. Skip or Buy shows you the real number before you buy.
problemBridge.bootsTheory: As Terry Pratchett wrote: a man who buys cheap boots spends more over a lifetime than a man who buys good ones. The price tag punishes those who cannot see past it.
